purpose: Research-backed list of additional tools worth installing or activating for MetaBlooms OS work.

ranked_install_candidates.why: Fast linting and formatting; catches many script bugs before execution and can replace a pile of smaller Python lint/format tools.
evidence: Ruff official docs describe it as an extremely fast Python linter and formatter written in Rust.
Ruff formatter docs describe it as a fast formatter designed as a drop-in replacement for Black.
ranked_install_candidates.recommended_governance: Install/vendor ruff and require ruff check on generated/modified Python scripts before commit.

ranked_install_candidates.why: Static type checking for Python; especially useful for registry/mutation engine code where type mistakes cause governance bugs.
evidence: Microsoft Pyright docs describe it as a full-featured standards-compliant static type checker designed for high performance and large Python code bases.
ranked_install_candidates.recommended_governance: Install via npm or vendor CLI; run pyright on 0_kernel/scripts after structural patches.

ranked_install_candidates.why: The Python module exists, but browser runtime may need installation. This gives actual browser validation for HTML learning engines instead of file-exists claims.
evidence: Playwright supports Chromium, Firefox and WebKit with one API and is available for Python.
Playwright install docs require browser installation after package install.
ranked_install_candidates.recommended_governance: Add browser-runtime probe; if install succeeds, require smoke test for downloadable HTML engines.

ranked_install_candidates.why: In-process analytics database for large audit tables, registries, CSVs, telemetry, and duplicate analysis.
evidence: DuckDB is described as an in-process analytical database suitable for fast local analytics and data processing workflows.
ranked_install_candidates.recommended_governance: Install if registry/log volume grows; use for audit ledgers and telemetry.

ranked_install_candidates.why: Multi-language parsing for JS/HTML/CSS/Python-like source. Useful later for richer multi-language engine patching.
evidence: Tree-sitter is an incremental parsing system that builds concrete syntax trees and is useful for syntax highlighting, go-to definition, code reshaping, and related programming tools.
ranked_install_candidates.recommended_governance: Defer until JS/HTML codemod need exceeds bs4/lxml/Node.

ranked_install_candidates.why: Already available; stronger typed validation for request objects, receipts, and policies than manual dict checks.
evidence: Pydantic docs describe it as a widely used Python data validation library that uses type annotations to parse and validate complex data.
ranked_install_candidates.recommended_governance: Start using pydantic models for new Python governance engines.

ranked_install_candidates.why: Already available; creates a real regression harness instead of one-off manual probes.
evidence: pytest is widely documented as a framework for writing scalable tests with simple syntax.
ranked_install_candidates.recommended_governance: Create 0_kernel/tests and require pytest after changes to scripts.

ranked_install_candidates.why: Already available. Fast Python package/project manager; useful for dependency bootstrap and repeatable vendor installs.
evidence: uv official docs describe it as an extremely fast Python package and project manager written in Rust.
ranked_install_candidates.recommended_governance: Prefer uv for future package bootstrap when compatible; fall back to pip.

policy_update_needed.new_rule: Before coding/export/HTML/test-heavy work, check installable tool policy and prefer the ranked authoritative tool over ad hoc scripts.
